self_report.reflection: Substitute t = u^2 to get I = 2∫_0^∞ e^{-a(u^2+1/u^2)}du = 2e^{-2a}∫_0^∞ e^{-a(u-1/u)^2}du, using u^2+1/u^2 = (u-1/u)^2+2. Splitting at u=1 and applying u→1/w on [0,1] folds the integral to ∫_1^∞(1+1/u^2)e^{-a(u-1/u)^2}du; then v=u-1/u gives ∫_0^∞ e^{-av^2}dv = (1/2)√(π/a). So I = √(π/a)·e^{-2a}, matching the claim including the a=1985 case.
self_report.notes: Standard Putnam 1985 B-5 derivation reproduces the asserted closed form exactly.
